title: Agrostis pseudopungens
taxonomy: Agrostis pseudopungens is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This plant belongs to the genus Agrostis, which includes various species of bentgrasses. Agrostis pseudopungens is further grouped into the tribe Poeae, a subfamily of grasses characterized by spikelets with two florets. The species is also classified as being native to the mountainous regions of eastern Asia. Agrostis pseudopungens is a type of perennial grass that can be found growing in high-altitude areas.

intro: Agrostis pseudopungens, commonly known as False Bunchgrass, is a perennial grass species native to certain regions of the world. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Agrostis pseudopungens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions.
